content:
- All required gates are green: docs-build, docs-hygiene, check, test, test-long, and lint.
- Marked the docs-tree task completed with passes=true and checked acceptance boxes, including explicit test-long evidence.
- Ran .ralph/task_switch.sh successfully; next is final git status review, commit all changes including Ralph bookkeeping, and push.

- Task 03 is fully complete and pushed as commit 20a1873.
- Final work: moved Start Here and Glossary into chapter-local paths, updated SUMMARY, removed stale empty docs/src dirs, marked the task completed/passing, ran task_switch, committed all Ralph/docs changes, and pushed.
- Final verification was green: make docs-build, make docs-hygiene, make check, make test, make test-long, make lint; post-commit docs publish also succeeded.

- Read prior progress for Choose-Task; no prior log content was returned.
- Inspected .ralph/current_tasks.md and .ralph/current_tasks_done.md tags to determine selectable work by precedence.
- Current active backlog appears to be two failing unblocked non-bug tasks plus one recurring meta-task; next I need the exact task file path from current_tasks.md and then write it into .ralph/current_task.txt.

- Opened the generated current task summary and extracted the exact candidate task paths.
- Applied selection precedence: no active failing bugs, no blockers on active tasks, so the highest-precedence failing non-bug task is the README task because the only other failing task is explicitly low priority.
- Wrote .ralph/tasks/story-docs-useful-guides/04-task-create-repo-readme.md into .ralph/current_task.txt and stopped immediately after selection.

- Read startup progress log and current task pointer.
- Active task is .ralph/tasks/story-docs-useful-guides/04-task-create-repo-readme.md.
- Reading the task file now to determine whether the required state is planning, skeptical review, or execution.

- Read the active README task and confirmed it was still in the initial planning state.
- Gathered minimal repo context from Cargo.toml, Makefile, docs SUMMARY, Start Here, and Quick Start so the task plan could reference exact docs and gate targets.
- Wrote a detailed execution plan into .ralph/tasks/story-docs-useful-guides/04-task-create-repo-readme.md and ended it with TO BE VERIFIED as instructed.
- Must stop now. Next engineer should do a deep skeptical review, alter at least one plan item, and only then replace the marker with NOW EXECUTE.

- Read the current README task and confirmed it was sitting at TO BE VERIFIED.
- Did a skeptical review against the real repo layout: checked docs/src/SUMMARY.md, docs/src/start-here/index.md, docs/src/quick-start/index.md, and Makefile.
- The original plan needed at least one concrete change: it did not explicitly cover GitHub-facing README link targets or docs build/hygiene verification for the new front-door doc. Next I am updating the task plan to fix that and then flipping the marker to NOW EXECUTE.

- Finished the skeptical review phase for the README task.
- Updated .ralph/tasks/story-docs-useful-guides/04-task-create-repo-readme.md by changing the execution plan in concrete ways: added explicit GitHub-root link validation for README targets and added docs-build/docs-hygiene alongside the required gates.
- Replaced TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE and stopped before implementation so the next pass can execute the plan directly without more exploration.

- Confirmed the active task file is .ralph/tasks/story-docs-useful-guides/04-task-create-repo-readme.md and it is already in NOW EXECUTE state.
- Starting execution directly per task instructions: gather only the plan-required repo/docs entry points, use parallel subagents for bounded read-only support, then draft README and run the full gate set.
- Must remember to update task checkboxes, set passes=true only after all gates are green, then task_switch, commit, and push.

- Read startup progress and began bounded README link verification.
- Inspecting SUMMARY and newcomer landing pages to extract exact GitHub-root labels/paths.
- Need to confirm any directly linked landing pages before reporting final README targets.

- Read startup progress log to pick up the active README task context.
- Starting a bounded read-only inventory limited to repository root entries and Makefile targets for newcomer-facing guidance.
- Next: extract the highest-signal commands, entry points, and caveats without reading into subdirectories.

- Root inventory confirms the main newcomer-facing entry points are Cargo.toml, Makefile, docs/, src/, tests/, docker/, and .env.docker.example.
- Read Makefile fully; it defines the canonical gates and local/docker workflows, with strict prereq checks and anti-dry-run guards.
- Reading the root Cargo.toml now only to confirm the crate/workspace shape before writing the final concise handoff.

- Verified SUMMARY labels for Start Here and Quick Start.
- Checking directly referenced landing pages: Operator Guide and Start Here docs map guidance.
- Final answer should include exact repo-root markdown link targets only; no edits made.

- Confirmed from root Cargo.toml that this is a single crate package named pgtuskmaster_rust, not a multi-crate workspace.
- Ready to hand back a concise newcomer-facing inventory focused on canonical make targets, root entry points, and setup caveats.
- No files edited; scope stayed bounded to repository root artifacts plus Makefile as requested.

- Finished bounded read-only verification of README-facing docs links.
- Confirmed exact root-relative targets for Start Here, Quick Start, Operator Guide, and optional Reading Paths And Book Map.
- No files edited; returning paste-ready labels/paths for GitHub root README use.

- Read the exact doc chapters named in the plan plus the Makefile and quick-start pages.
- README needs to present the project as a local PostgreSQL HA controller, point first to the checked-in Docker Compose quick start, and link from the repo root to docs/src chapter landing pages.
- Waiting on the two bounded read-only subagents to confirm the most important commands and the exact GitHub-friendly doc link targets before editing files.

- Added a new root README.md.
- README presents pgtuskmaster_rust as a conservative local PostgreSQL HA controller, points newcomers to the container-first quick start, lists the key make targets, and links to docs/src landing pages that work from the repo root on GitHub.
- Next is full gate execution: docs-build, docs-hygiene, check, test, test-long, lint; then task-file updates, task switch, commit, and push if all green.

- docs-build, docs-hygiene, check, and the default test suite are green.
- make test completed with 428 passed and 9 skipped by the default nextest profile filter.
- Starting make test-long now; after that I still need make lint, task file updates, task switch, commit, and push.

- make test-long passed completely: 9 ultra-long tests green, then compose-config, smoke-single, and smoke-cluster all green.
- The only remaining required gate is make lint.
- If lint stays green, next steps are updating the task file checkboxes/status/passes, running task_switch, committing all changes including .ralph evidence/bookkeeping, and pushing.

- make lint passed, so every required gate is now green: docs-build, docs-hygiene, check, test, test-long, lint.
- Preparing the final task-file update now: tick acceptance boxes, set status done, set passes=true, and record the extra docs-gate evidence since the user requires it even though the acceptance block only lists check/test/lint.
- After that I must run .ralph/task_switch.sh, commit everything including Ralph files/evidence, and push.
